articles.title: Roadworks on Birch Road in Wardle Scheduled for July 2026
articles.excerpt: Roadworks on Birch Road in Wardle will take place from 20 July 2026, resulting in temporary closures and restrictions.
articles.content_html:
A roadworks order will be implemented on Birch Road in Wardle from 20 July 2026. The works are expected to take place between 8am and 6pm on two days, which will be confirmed based on weather conditions.

The closure will affect part of Birch Road, starting at the junction with Ramsden Road and extending 1,236 metres in a south-easterly direction. During this period, no waiting will be permitted in the affected area.

Advance warning signs will be displayed on site two weeks prior to the commencement of the works, indicating the exact dates. Residents and motorists are advised to plan their journeys accordingly during this time.

Alternative routes will be available via Ramsden Road, Wardle Road, and Halifax Road (A58). For further details, a map of roadworks across Greater Manchester can be viewed online.

articles.title: Edenfield Road to Close for Roadworks from 15 August 2026
articles.excerpt: Edenfield Road in Rochdale will be temporarily closed for roadworks starting on 15 August 2026, affecting traffic flow in the area.
articles.content_html:
Part of Edenfield Road will be temporarily closed from 15 August 2026. The closure will begin 10 metres west of the junction with Overtown Lane and extend 674 metres in an easterly direction.

The roadworks are expected to impact traffic flow on Edenfield Road and surrounding routes, including Spotland Road, Sandy Lane, and Bury Road.

Drivers may need to seek alternative routes during this period. Further details about the roadworks and affected areas can be found on the Rochdale Borough Council website.

Residents and commuters are advised to plan their journeys accordingly as the road closure may lead to delays.

articles.title: Meeting Requested to Discuss Hopwood Triangle Road Closure
articles.excerpt: A meeting has been requested with the Highways team and local councillors to address the impact of the Hopwood Triangle road closure in Heywood.
articles.content_html:
A meeting has been requested with the Highways team, including all councillors in Heywood, to discuss the Hopwood Triangle scheme. The request aims to address the significant impact the associated road closure has had on the town.

The closure has raised concerns among local residents and businesses, prompting the need for a collaborative discussion on the matter. The meeting is expected to focus on the implications of the road closure and potential solutions.

Details regarding the date and time of the meeting have not yet been confirmed. However, it is anticipated that the discussions will involve various stakeholders affected by the changes in traffic flow.

Further updates will be provided as more information becomes available regarding the meeting and any decisions made thereafter.

articles.title: Roadworks Cause Delays on A627(M) Southbound Near Rochdale
articles.excerpt: Planned roadworks on the A627(M) southbound are causing delays between A664 and the M62, affecting traffic flow in the area.
articles.content_html:
Planned roadworks on the A627(M) southbound are causing delays between the A664 near Rochdale and the M62. The works began on 18 July 2026 and are part of ongoing maintenance efforts to improve the highway.

Motorists are advised to allow extra time for their journeys and to follow any diversion signs in the area. Local authorities have not specified the duration of the roadworks.

Drivers should check traffic reports for the latest information before travelling. The roadworks are expected to impact traffic flow significantly.

articles.title: Traffic Disruption in Heywood Due to Middleton Road Changes
articles.excerpt: Traffic in Heywood has been severely disrupted today, partly due to the ongoing pedestrianisation of Middleton Road, affecting routes throughout the town.
articles.content_html:
Traffic in Heywood has been severely disrupted today, partly due to the ongoing pedestrianisation of Middleton Road. Reports indicate that the changes are causing significant congestion across various routes in the town.

The alterations to Middleton Road are part of an experimental scheme aimed at improving pedestrian access. However, some residents have questioned the timing of the changes, particularly as other roadworks are also taking place in the area.

Witnesses described the situation as chaotic, with delays affecting not only the immediate vicinity of the 'Hopwood Triangle' but also extending throughout Heywood. The combination of roadworks and the new pedestrianisation measures has led to frustration among drivers and pedestrians alike.

Authorities have not yet commented on the situation or provided information regarding potential adjustments to the traffic management plan.

articles.title: Officers Conduct Off-Road Bike Operation in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: Officers from Rochdale North Neighbourhood, supported by various agencies, carried out an operation targeting anti-social behaviour involving off-road bikes.
articles.content_html:
Officers and PCSOs from Rochdale North Neighbourhood recently conducted a dedicated operation to address anti-social behaviour related to off-road bikes.

The operation was supported by colleagues from Rochdale East, the Special Constabulary, community volunteers, and United Utilities.

This initiative aimed to tackle issues arising from the use of off-road bikes in the area, which have been a concern for local residents.

Authorities continue to monitor the situation and encourage community involvement in reporting any further incidents.

articles.title: Road Closures Announced for Rochdale Feel Good Festival
articles.excerpt: Road closures will be in effect in Rochdale from 7 August 2026 for the Rochdale Feel Good Festival, according to Rochdale Borough Council.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Borough Council has announced that road closures will take place in Rochdale from 7 August 2026 for the Rochdale Feel Good Festival.

The festival is expected to attract a large number of visitors, which will necessitate the temporary closure of certain roads to ensure public safety and smooth traffic flow.

Details regarding specific road closures and diversions will be provided closer to the event date, allowing residents and visitors to plan their journeys accordingly.

People can check official council communications for updates on the festival and related traffic changes.

articles.title: Residents on Railway Street Concerned About Traffic Issues
articles.excerpt: Residents on Railway Street are encouraged to contact Rochdale Highways regarding traffic problems caused by drivers ignoring road closure signs on Green Lane.
articles.content_html:
Residents on Railway Street have been advised to report ongoing traffic issues to Rochdale Highways. Concerns have been raised about drivers ignoring road closure signs on Green Lane, leading to increased traffic on Railway Street.

A post on social media highlighted the frustrations of local residents, who described the situation as a 'rat run' due to the disregard for the road closures. Residents are encouraged to voice their concerns by calling Rochdale Highways at 0300 303 8879.

The road closures on Green Lane are intended to manage traffic flow and ensure safety during ongoing works. However, the failure of some drivers to adhere to the signs has resulted in significant disruption for those living nearby.

Local authorities are expected to monitor the situation and may take further action to address the traffic problems if they persist.

articles.title: Crash Closes Heywood Old Road in Middleton
articles.excerpt: Crews are responding to a crash on Heywood Old Road in Middleton, which has resulted in the road being closed. Drivers are advised to avoid the area.
articles.content_html:
A crash has occurred on Heywood Old Road in Middleton, prompting crews to respond to the incident. The road is currently closed as emergency services manage the situation.

Drivers are advised to avoid the area while the response is underway. No further details about the crash have been released.

Authorities have not provided information regarding any injuries or the cause of the crash. Updates will follow as more information becomes available.

Motorists should seek alternative routes to prevent delays during the closure.

articles.title: Traffic Disruption Expected Following Steel Rescue Deal Costs Surpassing £600m
articles.excerpt: Traffic disruption is anticipated as costs for the steel rescue deal exceed £600 million, impacting local transport routes.
articles.content_html:
Traffic disruption is expected in Rochdale following reports that costs for the steel rescue deal have surpassed £600 million. The financial implications of this deal could affect local transport routes and services.

Details regarding specific routes or services impacted have not yet been disclosed, but local authorities are likely to assess the situation in the coming days.

Residents and commuters are advised to remain vigilant and check for updates regarding any changes to transport services as the situation develops.
